reviews.review_description:
Hello together, and thanks for playing Next Player Please!

Our vision with Next Player Please is to create an experience for a group of people, a social happening in which all can play in the VR world. For that, we use the VR headset as a looking glass that you pass from player to player. This way the focus stays on the social interaction around the table while adding interesting immersion and controls. The VR headset becomes a portable mini-game collection that can be always extended with more content and variations.

Next Player Please is currently in the beta and not feature complete yet. To get to this point, your feedback is very important to us. We are already working on new features like custom tournaments and more mini-games.

You can reach us by leaving a review with your suggestions and feedback or joining our discord server to get in contact: https://discord.gg/bQ4vD7DZ5m

We wish you a lot of fun on your Next Player Please game night with your friends and family!
Awesome collection of games with different mechanics to each game. My favorite one is "build and destroy". And you can play local multiplayer with only one Quest! Very unique idea, you need to try!

I am honored to be the first to review this. 

Quest does not really have a party game.  None that l am aware of for more than 2 people at least. 

This has now changed.

Think a bit of "Keep talking and nobody explodes"  but for more than two people, and in Wario Land party game  fashion too.

The passthrough used here is very expertly done. 

Admittedly l did not understand yet how to let the app see my hands instead of the controller as they advertize, but still. Even with just the controller, this is a great party game, heck it is the only party game.

Great quality mini games (10 of them). Great tutorials and very unique.

Get it while its free folks!

developer_response.body:
Hello and thank you very much for your review! About the hand tracking, if you switch in the option menu to "Hands" under interaction type  the tutorial shows you have to switch to it, or you can just start the game with hand tracking, and it is automatically set to it.
Best the NPP Team

reviews.review_title: First local MP VR game I've seen..
reviews.review_description: ...and I'm surprised why there aren't more. The concept is so simple and taking turns with one VR headset works great. The party games are easy to pick up and quite diverse. Passthrough lets you see your friends who are mocking you while you play. It's just fun!

reviews.review_title: Awesome game to play with friends!
reviews.review_description: The minigames are sooo much fun. Had some great laughs. And the fact that you share a single headset is super convenient and a really neat idea from the devs. Can’t wait to see how this game evolves and excited about future updates!

Next Player Please is a well designed Introduction experience if you want to Show off VR to your Friends and just as a Party Game in general. The diversity of modes is refreshing and the simplicity of the interaction lends itself well as a family entertainment option. A must have for Quest users.

This is pretty incredible! for the low low low price of $0 you can own a wonderful game that you can play with friends and family, it requires no setup other than the headset and a table, can you guess what its called? Next Player Please!
-Thank you devs, this game is super great, please keep working on this and improving it, I would probably pay for this game, but keeping it free would be even better.

reviews.review_title: One Quest for four players? What?
reviews.review_description:
That's a cool concept!

Fast-paced mini-games with enough time to switch between players.
Very funny. In some games, you compete, while in others, you cooperate. 

+ Very intuitive, with a proper explanation for each mini-game. 
+ You can draw where your play area (empty table) is.
+ Hand tracking works super well. 
+ You are never disoriented bc you see your whole room in other colors.

- The speaker's voice sounds artificial. (That can be a plus. I know we're in the future if we play something like this.)

Overall very cool casual game!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot about your feedback! Yes, the speaker's voice is an artificial voice, and we are looking forward to changing that in the future

reviews.review_description: First time I‘ve seen this kind of multiplayer concept and I didn‘t think it would work this well. There is some downtime for those not wearing the headset, but coop games make it playable even for those without, just by using the controller.
